sources: Microsoft New Future of Work Report 2025

traits_and_skills.name: Structured problem solving
traits_and_skills.description: Ability to break down complex problems into steps, plan methodically, and execute in an ordered way. Valued in roles that require clear processes and predictable outcomes.
traits_and_skills.how_measured_or_observed: Choices under ambiguity (plan-first vs try-first); preference for clear goals and deadlines; self-report and scenario-based questions about how they approach new problems.
question_hints: When facing a new problem, do you prefer to plan step-by-step or try things quickly?
Do you prefer clear goals and deadlines or more open-ended tasks?

traits_and_skills.name: Experimentation and risk tolerance
traits_and_skills.description: Comfort with trying things quickly, iterating, and tolerating uncertainty. Associated with learning new domains and adapting when routines are disrupted.
traits_and_skills.how_measured_or_observed: Willingness to try without full information; preference for novelty vs predictability; response to 'how do you feel about learning something completely new?'.
question_hints: Do you prefer to try something quickly and see what happens, or plan first?
Do you enjoy new risks and challenges or prefer predictable tasks?

traits_and_skills.name: Social collaboration and influence
traits_and_skills.description: Tendency to work with others, coordinate people, and influence outcomes through relationships and communication. Distinct from superficial intermediation; reflects genuine coordination and teamwork.
traits_and_skills.how_measured_or_observed: Preference for team vs solo work; tendency to 'talk it through with others'; choices about work environment (team-based, collaborative).
question_hints: Do you prefer working with others to solve problems or working independently?
When stuck, do you seek input from others or research alone?

traits_and_skills.name: Research and analytical depth
traits_and_skills.description: Tendency to gather information, look for similar examples, and analyze before acting. Can be routine (easily automated) or high-level (synthesis, judgment, taste).
traits_and_skills.how_measured_or_observed: Whether they 'research or look for similar examples' first; preference for data-driven decisions; depth of explanation when solving problems.
question_hints: Do you look for similar examples or research before acting?
How much do you rely on data vs intuition?

traits_and_skills.name: Technical / hands-on orientation
traits_and_skills.description: Affinity for building, coding, debugging, or working with systems and tools. Includes both routine implementation (automation-prone) and design/architecture (complementary to AI).
traits_and_skills.how_measured_or_observed: Interest in building, coding, or technical projects; preference for practical step-by-step problem solving; self-reported technical hobbies or coursework.
question_hints: Do you enjoy building, coding, or technical projects?
Do you prefer hands-on, logical tasks or more open-ended creative ones?

traits_and_skills.name: Creative and open-ended thinking
traits_and_skills.description: Comfort with ambiguity, imagination, storytelling, and tasks that lack a single right answer. Valued where human taste, novelty, and judgment matter.
traits_and_skills.how_measured_or_observed: Interest in arts, design, storytelling; preference for 'freedom to explore and experiment'; responses to open-ended scenarios.
question_hints: Do you prefer clear instructions or freedom to explore?
Do you enjoy imaginative or storytelling-style tasks?

traits_and_skills.name: Strategic planning and logic
traits_and_skills.description: Ability to think ahead, weigh options, and align actions with longer-term goals. Distinct from routine execution; involves judgment and prioritization.
traits_and_skills.how_measured_or_observed: Preference for puzzles, strategy, research; how they describe decision-making under constraints; interest in planning and logic.
question_hints: Do you enjoy puzzles, strategy, or logical thinking?
How do you balance short-term tasks with long-term goals?

traits_and_skills.name: Instruction clarity and literal communication
traits_and_skills.description: Ability to write and give clear, literal instructions that others (or AI agents) can follow without ambiguity. Critical for human-AI collaboration and delegation.
traits_and_skills.how_measured_or_observed: Ability to explain a process step-by-step; preference for clear vs vague wording; self-awareness of how others interpret their requests.
question_hints: When you explain a task to someone, do you tend to be very specific or leave room for interpretation?
Have you ever had to give instructions to a tool or system? How did you approach it?

traits_and_skills.name: Delegation and oversight calibration
traits_and_skills.description: Ability to assign work to others (or agents), set expectations, and adjust oversight based on task complexity—more oversight for sensitive or strategic work, less for repetitive tasks.
traits_and_skills.how_measured_or_observed: Experience leading projects or coordinating others; how they describe dividing work; comfort with 'managing' rather than doing all work oneself.
question_hints: When working in a team, do you prefer to do things yourself or delegate and review?
How do you decide how closely to supervise someone else's work?

traits_and_skills.name: Feedback and refinement
traits_and_skills.description: Tendency to give constructive feedback, refine others' output, and improve quality through iteration. Important for managing AI agents and improving outcomes.
traits_and_skills.how_measured_or_observed: Whether they 'challenge with open-ended questions' to improve output; how they handle suboptimal work from others; preference for iteration vs one-shot delivery.
question_hints: When someone's work isn't quite right, do you fix it yourself or give feedback and ask for revisions?
Do you prefer to iterate on a solution or get it right the first time?

traits_and_skills.name: Adaptability and learning capacity
traits_and_skills.description: Willingness and ability to learn new tools, domains, and ways of working as the environment changes. Key for resilience when roles and tasks shift due to AI.
traits_and_skills.how_measured_or_observed: Response to 'how do you feel about learning something completely new?'; history of switching domains or tools; comfort with 'building on what I know' vs 'seeking new challenges'.
question_hints: Do you seek out new challenges or prefer to deepen existing skills?
How quickly do you adopt new tools or methods at work or school?

traits_and_skills.name: Judgment and taste
traits_and_skills.description: Ability to make nuanced decisions when there is no clear rule or data answer; includes aesthetic, ethical, or strategic 'taste' that is hard to automate.
traits_and_skills.how_measured_or_observed: How they handle decisions with no single right answer; preference for 'it depends on context'; examples of choosing between good options.
question_hints: When two options both seem good, how do you decide?
Can you think of a time when the 'right' answer wasn't obvious from data alone?

traits_and_skills.name: Motivation drivers
traits_and_skills.description: What energizes the person: goals, mastery, recognition, helping others, novelty, stability, etc. Informs fit for roles that remain human-centric vs those that are being automated.
traits_and_skills.how_measured_or_observed: Self-report on what makes work satisfying; choice of activities in free time; alignment with gaming/competition, social, creative, strategic, or neutral clusters.
question_hints: What kind of work or activities make you feel most engaged?
Do you prefer competition and goals or collaboration and helping others?

traits_and_skills.name: Routine execution and process adherence
traits_and_skills.description: Ability to perform repetitive, well-defined tasks consistently. Often highly automatable; value depends on whether the role is purely routine or includes variation.
traits_and_skills.how_measured_or_observed: Preference for 'clear instructions with no games or stories'; comfort with repetitive workflows; self-report on liking predictable, step-by-step work.
question_hints: Do you prefer predictable, step-by-step tasks or varied, novel ones?
How do you feel about doing the same type of task repeatedly?

traits_and_skills.name: Intermediation and friction-based value
traits_and_skills.description: Roles or behaviors that add value mainly by reducing others' friction (e.g. navigating complexity, handling tedious processes). Highly exposed when AI can remove that friction.
traits_and_skills.how_measured_or_observed: Whether they describe their value as 'helping others navigate complexity' or 'managing tedious processes'; orientation toward efficiency vs relationship-building in transactions.
question_hints: Do you see yourself as someone who 'saves others time' by handling details?
How much of your ideal job would be coordinating or navigating bureaucracy for others?
